playbooks.title: Phishing Credential Harvest Playbook
playbooks.purpose: Detailed tabletop response playbook for Phishing Credential Harvest.

steps.name: Detection
steps.description: Identify indicators of compromise from alerts, logs, or user reports.
steps.why: Early detection allows faster containment and limits impact.

steps.name: Triage
steps.description: Validate the alert and determine if the activity is malicious.
steps.why: Prevents wasted effort on false positives and prioritizes real threats.

steps.name: Scope Analysis
steps.description: Determine affected systems, users, and data.
steps.why: Understanding scope helps plan containment actions.

steps.name: Containment
steps.description: Isolate affected systems or accounts to prevent spread.
steps.why: Stops attackers from expanding their access.

steps.name: Investigation
steps.description: Collect logs, analyze indicators, and identify root cause.
steps.why: Determines how the incident occurred.

steps.name: Eradication
steps.description: Remove malware, revoke access, or patch vulnerabilities.
steps.why: Ensures attacker persistence mechanisms are eliminated.

steps.name: Recovery
steps.description: Restore systems and validate they are functioning normally.
steps.why: Returns business operations to normal.

steps.name: Communication
steps.description: Notify leadership and impacted stakeholders.
steps.why: Ensures proper awareness and decision-making.

steps.name: Documentation
steps.description: Record all actions taken and timeline of events.
steps.why: Creates audit trail and supports lessons learned.

steps.name: After Action Review
steps.description: Evaluate response effectiveness and improve procedures.
steps.why: Strengthens future incident response capabilities.

prompts: What indicators first confirmed the incident?
What containment action reduced risk fastest?
Which systems, users, or data were affected?
What should change in the playbook after this exercise?

steps.description: Determine affected systems, users, and data.
steps.why: Understanding scope helps plan containment actions.

steps.name: Containment
steps.description: Isolate affected systems or accounts to prevent spread.

steps.why: Determines how the incident occurred.

steps.name: Eradication
steps.description: Remove malware, revoke access, or patch vulnerabilities.
steps.why: Ensures attacker persistence mechanisms are eliminated.

steps.name: Recovery
steps.description: Restore systems and validate they are functioning normally.
steps.why: Returns business operations to normal.

steps.name: Communication
steps.description: Notify leadership and impacted stakeholders.
steps.why: Ensures proper awareness and decision-making.

steps.name: Documentation
steps.description: Record all actions taken and timeline of events.
steps.why: Creates audit trail and supports lessons learned.

steps.why: Strengthens future incident response capabilities.
